behavior_summary: U8P_JUG::look is the 'U8P_JUG::look' (examine) handler for the NPC/object 'Jug'.
When examined, the handler emits a single short bark/text: "jug ".
Immediately after the bark the process executes a suspend, yielding/pausing the U8P_JUG::look process.
No conditionals, menu logic, item checks, global flag reads, or writes are present — this is a minimal examine response.
notable_control_flow: Straight-line sequence: bark (player-facing text) -> suspend (end/yield of the U8P_JUG::look process).
No branching or calls; control does not modify game state or global flags.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "jug " intentional or an artifact of extraction?
Could U8P_JUG::look also be invoked by an engine fast-area entry (enterFastArea) aside from the explicit player 'U8P_JUG::look' action? (This is plausible but not present in the extracted nodes.)

main_function.name: U8P_JUG::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the player's examine/U8P_JUG::look action by showing a short description (bark) for Jug and then suspending the process.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player performs the 'U8P_JUG::look' (examine) action on Jug.
May also be triggered by the engine's fast-area/interaction region for this actor (inferred).
